revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]" to "<nowiki>[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]</nowiki>"

revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{tsh|Iso2lang|Langname|Language|Lname|Lcode}} {{May be substituted}} {{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:ISO 639 name}} {{Language templates}} This template may be used to convert a language name (e.g. 'Spanish' or 'German') to an [[ISO 639]] language code ('es' or 'de', respectively) and vice-versa, or to determine if a given code or language is valid in ISO 639. == Usage == * {{tnull | ISO 639 name | ''ISO code''}} * {{tnull | ISO 639 name | fn{{=}}iso_639_code_to_name | ''ISO code''}} * {{tnull | Iso 639 name | fn{{=}}iso_639_name_to_code | ''Language name''}} * {{tnull | ISO 639 name | fn{{=}}iso_639 | ''ISO code or language name'' | link{{=}}yes| hide-err{{=}}yes }} * {{tnull | iso 639 name | fn{{=}}iso_639_code_exists | ''ISO code''}} == Parameters == There is one required parameter and four optional parameters: * {{para|1}} – unnamed (positional) param: either an ISO 639 language code, or an ISO 639 language name (required) * {{para|fn}} – function name: identifies what to do with the code or name in param 1 (convert it, check if it exists; etc.) (optional). These are the most commonly used values: *:* {{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}} – convert the ISO 639 code in param 1 to a language name. (This is the default if {{kbd|fn}} is omitted.) *:* {{pval|iso_639_name_to_code}} – convert the language name in param 1 to an ISO 639 code *:* {{pval|iso_639_code_exists}} – return {{kbd|true}} if the value in param 1 is an ISO 639 code, otherwise empty *:* {{pval|iso_639_name_exists}} – return {{kbd|true}} if the value in param 1 is an ISO 639 language name, otherwise empty *:* {{pval|iso_639}} – try to convert the value in param 1 from a language name to an ISO 639 code, and if that fails then try the other way (code to language) *: (For more values of {{para|fn}}, see {{slink||Additional function values}} below). * {{para|link|yes}} – if a language name is returned, links it to the Wikipedia article about the language (optional, default=no) * {{para|label|''any string''}} – if a language name is returned, and linked with {{para|link|yes}}, then you can alter the link text by setting this param * {{para|hide_err|yes}} – blocks the error message returned upon failure of any of the three conversion functions ({{hairspace}}{{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}}, {{pval|iso_639_name_to_code}}, {{pval|iso_639}}{{hairspace}}) (optional; default=no) === Additional function values === The following function values are also available for parameter {{para|fn}}: * {{pval|iso_639_code_1_to_name}} – same as {{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}} except constrained to the [[ISO 639-1]] data table * {{pval|iso_639_code_2_to_name}} – same as {{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}} except constrained to the [[ISO 639-2]] data table * {{pval|iso_639_code_2B_to_name}} – same as {{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}} except constrained to the [[ISO 639-2B]] data table * {{pval|iso_639_code_3_to_name}} – same as {{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}} except constrained to the [[ISO 639-3]] data table * {{pval|iso_639_code_5_to_name}} – same as {{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}} except constrained to the [[ISO 639-5]] data table == Language names and codes == For a list of language names and ISO-639 codes, see: * [[List of Wikipedias]] * [[Special:SiteMatrix]] * [[Lists of ISO 639 codes]] == Examples == === One parameter === * {{tlx|ISO 639 name|en|_show_result=y}} * {{tlx|ISO 639 name|fr|_show_result=y}} === With |fn=iso_639_name_to_code === * {{tlx|ISO 639 name|English|fn{{=}}iso_639_name_to_code|_show_result=y}} * {{tlx|ISO 639 name|French|fn{{=}}iso_639_name_to_code|_show_result=y}} ==Notes== The {{tlx|ISO 639 name}} template will trim extraneous [[IETF language tag]]s to just the ISO 639 code. E.g.: Sanskrit in Latin script, which IETF language tag is sa-Latn, renders as: * {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn}} → {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|cat=no}} The error message can be suppressed with {{para|hide-err|yes}}: * {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes}} → {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes}} The rendered language name can be linked to the appropriate language article with {{para|link|yes}}: * {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes|link=yes}} → {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes|link=yes}} When linking, an alternate link label can be provided with {{para|label|<{{var|label text}}>}}: * {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes|link=yes|label=sa-Latn}} → {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes|link=yes|label=sa-Latn}} The {{tlx|ISO 639 name link}} template is the same as {{tnull|ISO 639 name}} except that it presets {{para|link|yes}}. Both of these templates {{em|can}} be safely substituted. ==Error messages== This template produces error messages. These messages are: *{{error-small|<{{var|code}}> is an IETF tag}} – [[IETF language tag]]s are not ISO 639 language codes. When these tags are encountered, the template attempts to render the correct language and also emits this error message *{{error-small|ISO 639-<{{var|part}}> code is required}} – where <{{var|part}}> is 1, 2, 3, 5; template was called without the required language code *{{error-small|<{{var|code}}> is not an ISO 639-<{{var|part}}> code}} – <{{var|code}}> fails simple length validation; 639 part 1, language codes are two characters, for 639 parts 2, 3, and 5, language codes are three characters *{{error-small|<{{var|code}}> not found in ISO 639-<{{var|part}}> list}} – <{{var|code}}> not found after searching ISO 639-<{{var|part}}> data The module that does the work for this template has other functionality not supported by this template. See [[Module:ISO 639 name]]. Error messages related to that other functionality are: *{{error-small|language name required}} – module function iso_639_name_to_code() only – this message emitted when a language name is not provided in the {{#invoke:}} *{{error-small|<{{var|part}}> not an ISO 639 part}} – module functions iso_639_name_to_code() and iso_639() only – this message emitted when <{{var|part}}> is not one of 1, 2, 3, or 5 *{{error-small|language code or name required}} – module function iso_639() only – this message emitted when a language name or a language code is not provided in the {{#invoke:}} == Tracking categories == * {{Category link with count|ISO 639 name template errors}} == See also == * [[:Category:Lang-x templates]] * [[:Category:Lang-x templates with other than ISO 639]] * [[:Category:Articles containing non-English-language text]] * {{tl|lang}} * {{tl|mw lang}} * {{tl|language with name}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:ISO 639 templates| ]] [[Category:Language tag handling templates]] }}

Error messages related to that other functionality are: *{{error-small|language name required}} – module function iso_639_name_to_code() only – this message emitted when a language name is not provided in the {{#invoke:}} *{{error-small|<{{var|part}}> not an ISO 639 part}} – module functions iso_639_name_to_code() and iso_639() only – this message emitted when <{{var|part}}> is not one of 1, 2, 3, or 5 *{{error-small|language code or name required}} – module function iso_639() only – this message emitted when a language name or a language code is not provided in the {{#invoke:}} == Tracking categories == * {{Category link with count|ISO 639 name template errors}} == See also == * [[:Category:Lang-x templates]] * [[:Category:Lang-x templates with other than ISO 639]] * [[:Category:Articles containing non-English-language text]] * {{tl|lang}} * {{tl|mw lang}} * {{tl|language with name}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:ISO 639 templates| ]] [[Category:Language tag handling templates]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{tsh|Iso2lang|Langname|Language|Lname|Lcode}} {{May be substituted}} {{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:ISO 639 name}} {{Language templates}} This template may be used to convert a language name (e.g. 'Spanish' or 'German') to an [[:en:ISO 639|ISO 639]] language code ('es' or 'de', respectively) and vice-versa, or to determine if a given code or language is valid in ISO 639. == Usage == * {{tnull | ISO 639 name | ''ISO code''}} * {{tnull | ISO 639 name | fn{{=}}iso_639_code_to_name | ''ISO code''}} * {{tnull | Iso 639 name | fn{{=}}iso_639_name_to_code | ''Language name''}} * {{tnull | ISO 639 name | fn{{=}}iso_639 | ''ISO code or language name'' | link{{=}}yes| hide-err{{=}}yes }} * {{tnull | iso 639 name | fn{{=}}iso_639_code_exists | ''ISO code''}} == Parameters == There is one required parameter and four optional parameters: * {{para|1}} – unnamed (positional) param: either an ISO 639 language code, or an ISO 639 language name (required) * {{para|fn}} – function name: identifies what to do with the code or name in param 1 (convert it, check if it exists; etc.) (optional). These are the most commonly used values: *:* {{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}} – convert the ISO 639 code in param 1 to a language name. (This is the default if {{kbd|fn}} is omitted.) *:* {{pval|iso_639_name_to_code}} – convert the language name in param 1 to an ISO 639 code *:* {{pval|iso_639_code_exists}} – return {{kbd|true}} if the value in param 1 is an ISO 639 code, otherwise empty *:* {{pval|iso_639_name_exists}} – return {{kbd|true}} if the value in param 1 is an ISO 639 language name, otherwise empty *:* {{pval|iso_639}} – try to convert the value in param 1 from a language name to an ISO 639 code, and if that fails then try the other way (code to language) *: (For more values of {{para|fn}}, see {{slink||Additional function values}} below). * {{para|link|yes}} – if a language name is returned, links it to the Wikipedia article about the language (optional, default=no) * {{para|label|''any string''}} – if a language name is returned, and linked with {{para|link|yes}}, then you can alter the link text by setting this param * {{para|hide_err|yes}} – blocks the error message returned upon failure of any of the three conversion functions ({{hairspace}}{{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}}, {{pval|iso_639_name_to_code}}, {{pval|iso_639}}{{hairspace}}) (optional; default=no) === Additional function values === The following function values are also available for parameter {{para|fn}}: * {{pval|iso_639_code_1_to_name}} – same as {{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}} except constrained to the [[:en:ISO 639-1|ISO 639-1]] data table * {{pval|iso_639_code_2_to_name}} – same as {{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}} except constrained to the [[ISO 639-2]] data table * {{pval|iso_639_code_2B_to_name}} – same as {{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}} except constrained to the [[ISO 639-2B]] data table * {{pval|iso_639_code_3_to_name}} – same as {{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}} except constrained to the [[:en:ISO 639-3|ISO 639-3]] data table * {{pval|iso_639_code_5_to_name}} – same as {{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}} except constrained to the [[ISO 639-5]] data table == Language names and codes == For a list of language names and ISO-639 codes, see: * [[List of Wikipedias]] * [[Special:SiteMatrix]] * [[Lists of ISO 639 codes]] == Examples == === One parameter === * {{tlx|ISO 639 name|en|_show_result=y}} * {{tlx|ISO 639 name|fr|_show_result=y}} === With |fn=iso_639_name_to_code === * {{tlx|ISO 639 name|English|fn{{=}}iso_639_name_to_code|_show_result=y}} * {{tlx|ISO 639 name|French|fn{{=}}iso_639_name_to_code|_show_result=y}} ==Notes== The {{tlx|ISO 639 name}} template will trim extraneous [[:en:IETF language tag|IETF language tag]]s to just the ISO 639 code. E.g.: Sanskrit in Latin script, which IETF language tag is sa-Latn, renders as: * {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn}} → {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|cat=no}} The error message can be suppressed with {{para|hide-err|yes}}: * {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes}} → {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes}} The rendered language name can be linked to the appropriate language article with {{para|link|yes}}: * {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes|link=yes}} → {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes|link=yes}} When linking, an alternate link label can be provided with {{para|label|<{{var|label text}}>}}: * {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes|link=yes|label=sa-Latn}} → {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes|link=yes|label=sa-Latn}} The {{tlx|ISO 639 name link}} template is the same as {{tnull|ISO 639 name}} except that it presets {{para|link|yes}}. Both of these templates {{em|can}} be safely substituted. ==Error messages== This template produces error messages. These messages are: *{{error-small|<{{var|code}}> is an IETF tag}} – [[:en:IETF language tag|IETF language tag]]s are not ISO 639 language codes. When these tags are encountered, the template attempts to render the correct language and also emits this error message *{{error-small|ISO 639-<{{var|part}}> code is required}} – where <{{var|part}}> is 1, 2, 3, 5; template was called without the required language code *{{error-small|<{{var|code}}> is not an ISO 639-<{{var|part}}> code}} – <{{var|code}}> fails simple length validation; 639 part 1, language codes are two characters, for 639 parts 2, 3, and 5, language codes are three characters *{{error-small|<{{var|code}}> not found in ISO 639-<{{var|part}}> list}} – <{{var|code}}> not found after searching ISO 639-<{{var|part}}> data The module that does the work for this template has other functionality not supported by this template. See [[Module:ISO 639 name]]. Error messages related to that other functionality are: *{{error-small|language name required}} – module function iso_639_name_to_code() only – this message emitted when a language name is not provided in the {{#invoke:}} *{{error-small|<{{var|part}}> not an ISO 639 part}} – module functions iso_639_name_to_code() and iso_639() only – this message emitted when <{{var|part}}> is not one of 1, 2, 3, or 5 *{{error-small|language code or name required}} – module function iso_639() only – this message emitted when a language name or a language code is not provided in the {{#invoke:}} == Tracking categories == * {{Category link with count|ISO 639 name template errors}} == See also == * [[:Category:Lang-x templates]] * [[:Category:Lang-x templates with other than ISO 639]] * [[:Category:Articles containing non-English-language text]] * {{tl|lang}} * {{tl|mw lang}} * {{tl|language with name}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:ISO 639 templates| ]] [[Category:Language tag handling templates]] }}

E.g.: Sanskrit in Latin script, which IETF language tag is sa-Latn, renders as: * {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn}} → {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|cat=no}} The error message can be suppressed with {{para|hide-err|yes}}: * {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes}} → {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes}} The rendered language name can be linked to the appropriate language article with {{para|link|yes}}: * {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes|link=yes}} → {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes|link=yes}} When linking, an alternate link label can be provided with {{para|label|<{{var|label text}}>}}: * {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes|link=yes|label=sa-Latn}} → {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes|link=yes|label=sa-Latn}} The {{tlx|ISO 639 name link}} template is the same as {{tnull|ISO 639 name}} except that it presets {{para|link|yes}}. Both of these templates {{em|can}} be safely substituted. ==Error messages== This template produces error messages. These messages are: *{{error-small|<{{var|code}}> is an IETF tag}} – [[:en:IETF language tag|IETF language tag]]s are not ISO 639 language codes. When these tags are encountered, the template attempts to render the correct language and also emits this error message *{{error-small|ISO 639-<{{var|part}}> code is required}} – where <{{var|part}}> is 1, 2, 3, 5; template was called without the required language code *{{error-small|<{{var|code}}> is not an ISO 639-<{{var|part}}> code}} – <{{var|code}}> fails simple length validation; 639 part 1, language codes are two characters, for 639 parts 2, 3, and 5, language codes are three characters *{{error-small|<{{var|code}}> not found in ISO 639-<{{var|part}}> list}} – <{{var|code}}> not found after searching ISO 639-<{{var|part}}> data The module that does the work for this template has other functionality not supported by this template. See [[Module:ISO 639 name]]. Error messages related to that other functionality are: *{{error-small|language name required}} – module function iso_639_name_to_code() only – this message emitted when a language name is not provided in the {{#invoke:}} *{{error-small|<{{var|part}}> not an ISO 639 part}} – module functions iso_639_name_to_code() and iso_639() only – this message emitted when <{{var|part}}> is not one of 1, 2, 3, or 5 *{{error-small|language code or name required}} – module function iso_639() only – this message emitted when a language name or a language code is not provided in the {{#invoke:}} == Tracking categories == * {{Category link with count|ISO 639 name template errors}} == See also == * [[:Category:Lang-x templates]] * [[:Category:Lang-x templates with other than ISO 639]] * [[:Category:Articles containing non-English-language text]] * {{tl|lang}} * {{tl|mw lang}} * {{tl|language with name}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:ISO 639 templates| ]] [[Category:Language tag handling templates]] }}

revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{tsh|Iso2lang|Langname|Language|Lname|Lcode}} {{May be substituted}} {{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:ISO 639 name}} {{Language templates}} This template may be used to convert a language name (e.g. 'Spanish' or 'German') to an [[:en:ISO 639|ISO 639]] language code ('es' or 'de', respectively) and vice-versa, or to determine if a given code or language is valid in ISO 639. == Usage == * {{tnull | ISO 639 name | ''ISO code''}} * {{tnull | ISO 639 name | fn{{=}}iso_639_code_to_name | ''ISO code''}} * {{tnull | Iso 639 name | fn{{=}}iso_639_name_to_code | ''Language name''}} * {{tnull | ISO 639 name | fn{{=}}iso_639 | ''ISO code or language name'' | link{{=}}yes| hide-err{{=}}yes }} * {{tnull | iso 639 name | fn{{=}}iso_639_code_exists | ''ISO code''}} == Parameters == There is one required parameter and four optional parameters: * {{para|1}} – unnamed (positional) param: either an ISO 639 language code, or an ISO 639 language name (required) * {{para|fn}} – function name: identifies what to do with the code or name in param 1 (convert it, check if it exists; etc.) (optional). These are the most commonly used values: *:* {{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}} – convert the ISO 639 code in param 1 to a language name. (This is the default if {{kbd|fn}} is omitted.) *:* {{pval|iso_639_name_to_code}} – convert the language name in param 1 to an ISO 639 code *:* {{pval|iso_639_code_exists}} – return {{kbd|true}} if the value in param 1 is an ISO 639 code, otherwise empty *:* {{pval|iso_639_name_exists}} – return {{kbd|true}} if the value in param 1 is an ISO 639 language name, otherwise empty *:* {{pval|iso_639}} – try to convert the value in param 1 from a language name to an ISO 639 code, and if that fails then try the other way (code to language) *: (For more values of {{para|fn}}, see {{slink||Additional function values}} below). * {{para|link|yes}} – if a language name is returned, links it to the Wikipedia article about the language (optional, default=no) * {{para|label|''any string''}} – if a language name is returned, and linked with {{para|link|yes}}, then you can alter the link text by setting this param * {{para|hide_err|yes}} – blocks the error message returned upon failure of any of the three conversion functions ({{hairspace}}{{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}}, {{pval|iso_639_name_to_code}}, {{pval|iso_639}}{{hairspace}}) (optional; default=no) === Additional function values === The following function values are also available for parameter {{para|fn}}: * {{pval|iso_639_code_1_to_name}} – same as {{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}} except constrained to the [[:en:ISO 639-1|ISO 639-1]] data table * {{pval|iso_639_code_2_to_name}} – same as {{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}} except constrained to the [[:en:ISO 639-2|ISO 639-2]] data table * {{pval|iso_639_code_2B_to_name}} – same as {{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}} except constrained to the [[ISO 639-2B]] data table * {{pval|iso_639_code_3_to_name}} – same as {{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}} except constrained to the [[:en:ISO 639-3|ISO 639-3]] data table * {{pval|iso_639_code_5_to_name}} – same as {{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}} except constrained to the [[:en:ISO 639-5|ISO 639-5]] data table == Language names and codes == For a list of language names and ISO-639 codes, see: * [[:en:List of Wikipedias|List of Wikipedias]] * [[Special:SiteMatrix]] * [[:en:Lists of ISO 639 codes|Lists of ISO 639 codes]] == Examples == === One parameter === * {{tlx|ISO 639 name|en|_show_result=y}} * {{tlx|ISO 639 name|fr|_show_result=y}} === With |fn=iso_639_name_to_code === * {{tlx|ISO 639 name|English|fn{{=}}iso_639_name_to_code|_show_result=y}} * {{tlx|ISO 639 name|French|fn{{=}}iso_639_name_to_code|_show_result=y}} ==Notes== The {{tlx|ISO 639 name}} template will trim extraneous [[:en:IETF language tag|IETF language tag]]s to just the ISO 639 code. E.g.: Sanskrit in Latin script, which IETF language tag is sa-Latn, renders as: * {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn}} → {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|cat=no}} The error message can be suppressed with {{para|hide-err|yes}}: * {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes}} → {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes}} The rendered language name can be linked to the appropriate language article with {{para|link|yes}}: * {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes|link=yes}} → {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes|link=yes}} When linking, an alternate link label can be provided with {{para|label|<{{var|label text}}>}}: * {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes|link=yes|label=sa-Latn}} → {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes|link=yes|label=sa-Latn}} The {{tlx|ISO 639 name link}} template is the same as {{tnull|ISO 639 name}} except that it presets {{para|link|yes}}. Both of these templates {{em|can}} be safely substituted. ==Error messages== This template produces error messages. revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{tsh|Iso2lang|Langname|Language|Lname|Lcode}} {{May be substituted}} {{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:ISO 639 name}} {{Language templates}} This template may be used to convert a language name (e.g. 'Spanish' or 'German') to an [[:en:ISO 639|ISO 639]] language code ('es' or 'de', respectively) and vice-versa, or to determine if a given code or language is valid in ISO 639. == Usage == * {{tnull | ISO 639 name | ''ISO code''}} * {{tnull | ISO 639 name | fn{{=}}iso_639_code_to_name | ''ISO code''}} * {{tnull | Iso 639 name | fn{{=}}iso_639_name_to_code | ''Language name''}} * {{tnull | ISO 639 name | fn{{=}}iso_639 | ''ISO code or language name'' | link{{=}}yes| hide-err{{=}}yes }} * {{tnull | iso 639 name | fn{{=}}iso_639_code_exists | ''ISO code''}} == Parameters == There is one required parameter and four optional parameters: * {{para|1}} – unnamed (positional) param: either an ISO 639 language code, or an ISO 639 language name (required) * {{para|fn}} – function name: identifies what to do with the code or name in param 1 (convert it, check if it exists; etc.) (optional). These are the most commonly used values: *:* {{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}} – convert the ISO 639 code in param 1 to a language name. (This is the default if {{kbd|fn}} is omitted.) *:* {{pval|iso_639_name_to_code}} – convert the language name in param 1 to an ISO 639 code *:* {{pval|iso_639_code_exists}} – return {{kbd|true}} if the value in param 1 is an ISO 639 code, otherwise empty *:* {{pval|iso_639_name_exists}} – return {{kbd|true}} if the value in param 1 is an ISO 639 language name, otherwise empty *:* {{pval|iso_639}} – try to convert the value in param 1 from a language name to an ISO 639 code, and if that fails then try the other way (code to language) *: (For more values of {{para|fn}}, see {{slink||Additional function values}} below). * {{para|link|yes}} – if a language name is returned, links it to the Wikipedia article about the language (optional, default=no) * {{para|label|''any string''}} – if a language name is returned, and linked with {{para|link|yes}}, then you can alter the link text by setting this param * {{para|hide_err|yes}} – blocks the error message returned upon failure of any of the three conversion functions ({{hairspace}}{{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}}, {{pval|iso_639_name_to_code}}, {{pval|iso_639}}{{hairspace}}) (optional; default=no) === Additional function values === The following function values are also available for parameter {{para|fn}}: * {{pval|iso_639_code_1_to_name}} – same as {{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}} except constrained to the [[:en:ISO 639-1|ISO 639-1]] data table * {{pval|iso_639_code_2_to_name}} – same as {{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}} except constrained to the [[:en:ISO 639-2|ISO 639-2]] data table * {{pval|iso_639_code_2B_to_name}} – same as {{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}} except constrained to the [[ISO 639-2B]] data table * {{pval|iso_639_code_3_to_name}} – same as {{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}} except constrained to the [[:en:ISO 639-3|ISO 639-3]] data table * {{pval|iso_639_code_5_to_name}} – same as {{pval|iso_639_code_to_name}} except constrained to the [[:en:ISO 639-5|ISO 639-5]] data table == Language names and codes == For a list of language names and ISO-639 codes, see: * [[:en:List of Wikipedias|List of Wikipedias]] * [[Special:SiteMatrix]] * [[:en:Lists of ISO 639 codes|Lists of ISO 639 codes]] == Examples == === One parameter === * {{tlx|ISO 639 name|en|_show_result=y}} * {{tlx|ISO 639 name|fr|_show_result=y}} === With |fn=iso_639_name_to_code === * {{tlx|ISO 639 name|English|fn{{=}}iso_639_name_to_code|_show_result=y}} * {{tlx|ISO 639 name|French|fn{{=}}iso_639_name_to_code|_show_result=y}} ==Notes== The {{tlx|ISO 639 name}} template will trim extraneous [[:en:IETF language tag|IETF language tag]]s to just the ISO 639 code. E.g.: Sanskrit in Latin script, which IETF language tag is sa-Latn, renders as: * {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn}} → {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|cat=no}} The error message can be suppressed with {{para|hide-err|yes}}: * {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes}} → {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes}} The rendered language name can be linked to the appropriate language article with {{para|link|yes}}: * {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes|link=yes}} → {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes|link=yes}} When linking, an alternate link label can be provided with {{para|label|<{{var|label text}}>}}: * {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes|link=yes|label=sa-Latn}} → {{ISO 639 name|sa-Latn|hide-err=yes|link=yes|label=sa-Latn}} The {{tlx|ISO 639 name link}} template is the same as {{tnull|ISO 639 name}} except that it presets {{para|link|yes}}. Both of these templates {{em|can}} be safely substituted. ==Error messages== This template produces error messages. These messages are: *{{error-small|<{{var|code}}> is an IETF tag}} – [[:en:IETF language tag|IETF language tag]]s are not ISO 639 language codes. When these tags are encountered, the template attempts to render the correct language and also emits this error message *{{error-small|ISO 639-<{{var|part}}> code is required}} – where <{{var|part}}> is 1, 2, 3, 5; template was called without the required language code *{{error-small|<{{var|code}}> is not an ISO 639-<{{var|part}}> code}} – <{{var|code}}> fails simple length validation; 639 part 1, language codes are two characters, for 639 parts 2, 3, and 5, language codes are three characters *{{error-small|<{{var|code}}> not found in ISO 639-<{{var|part}}> list}} – <{{var|code}}> not found after searching ISO 639-<{{var|part}}> data The module that does the work for this template has other functionality not supported by this template. See [[Module:ISO 639 name]]. Error messages related to that other functionality are: *{{error-small|language name required}} – module function iso_639_name_to_code() only – this message emitted when a language name is not provided in the {{#invoke:}} *{{error-small|<{{var|part}}> not an ISO 639 part}} – module functions iso_639_name_to_code() and iso_639() only – this message emitted when <{{var|part}}> is not one of 1, 2, 3, or 5 *{{error-small|language code or name required}} – module function iso_639() only – this message emitted when a language name or a language code is not provided in the {{#invoke:}} == Tracking categories == * {{Category link with count|ISO 639 name template errors}} == See also == * [[:Category:Lang-x templates]] * [[:Category:Lang-x templates with other than ISO 639]] * [[:Category:Articles containing non-English-language text]] * {{tl|lang}} * {{tl|mw lang}} * {{tl|language with name}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:ISO 639 templates| ]] [[Category:Language tag handling templates]] }} [[Category:Templates]]

Both of these templates {{em|can}} be safely substituted. ==Error messages== This template produces error messages. These messages are: *{{error-small|<{{var|code}}> is an IETF tag}} – [[:en:IETF language tag|IETF language tag]]s are not ISO 639 language codes.
